title: Installing OpenClaw
description: Step-by-step installation guide for macOS, Windows, and Linux.
content_mdx:
# Installing OpenClaw

Installing OpenClaw takes about 10-15 minutes. By the end of this module, you'll have OpenClaw installed and ready to start.

## Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have:

- Node.js version 18 or higher
- npm (comes with Node.js)
- A terminal / command prompt
- An API key from at least one AI provider (Anthropic, OpenAI, or OpenRouter)

## Installing Node.js

If you don't have Node.js installed:

**macOS:**
```bash
# Install via Homebrew (recommended)
brew install node

# Or download from nodejs.org
```

**Windows:**
```bash
# Download the installer from nodejs.org/en/download
# Or use winget:
winget install OpenJS.NodeJS
```

**Linux:**
```bash
# Ubuntu/Debian:
curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | sudo -E bash -
sudo apt-get install -y nodejs

# Arch Linux:
sudo pacman -S nodejs npm
```

## Verify Node.js Installation

```bash
node --version
# Should output: v20.x.x or higher

npm --version
# Should output: 10.x.x or higher
```

## Installing OpenClaw

```bash
npm install -g openclaw
```

This installs OpenClaw globally so you can run it from anywhere.

## Verify Installation

```bash
openclaw --version
```

You should see the OpenClaw version number.

## Getting an API Key

OpenClaw needs an AI provider API key. The easiest option is OpenRouter, which gives you access to 100+ models:

1. Go to https://openrouter.ai
2. Create a free account
3. Go to API Keys and create a new key
4. Copy the key (it starts with `sk-or-v1-`)

Alternatively, get an Anthropic API key:
1. Go to https://console.anthropic.com
2. Create an account and add a payment method
3. Go to API Keys and create a new key

## Initial Configuration

Run the setup wizard:

```bash
openclaw init
```

This will ask you:
- Your preferred AI provider and model
- Your API key
- Where to store your workspace

Answer the prompts and OpenClaw will create your initial configuration.

## Workspace Structure

After init, OpenClaw creates a workspace directory (default: `~/.openclaw/workspace`):

```
~/.openclaw/workspace/
    ├── SOUL.md          # Your agent's personality
    ├── USER.md          # Info about you
    ├── MEMORY.md        # Long-term memory
    └── memory/          # Daily notes
```

## Troubleshooting

**Permission errors on macOS/Linux:**
```bash
sudo npm install -g openclaw
# Or use a node version manager like nvm
```

**Windows PATH issues:**

If `openclaw` isn't recognized after installation, restart your terminal or add npm global bin to your PATH.

**Node version too old:**
```bash
# Install nvm to manage Node versions:
curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.0/install.sh | bash
nvm install 20
nvm use 20
```

## You're Ready!

OpenClaw is installed. In the next module, we'll start the Gateway and make sure everything is working.
